issues.description: Replacing `.unwrap_or(text)` with `.unwrap()` introduces a panic on malformed tree-sitter nodes. The original code had a comment explicitly warning that 'error recovery can produce malformed nodes', and a test `unclosed_string_does_not_panic` in edge_case_tests.rs validates this. The tree-sitter error-recovery path can produce string_literal nodes without matching quotes (e.g. unclosed strings), and `.unwrap()` will panic in that case. All three other quote-stripping sites in this file (lower_field_source at line 646, lower_meta_block at line 1663) consistently use `.unwrap_or()` for this reason.
issues.suggested_fix: Restore the safe fallback: `.unwrap_or(text)` instead of `.unwrap()`. This preserves the defensive pattern against tree-sitter error recovery.

issues.description: The new `lower_single_quoted_string` method uses `.unwrap()` on the strip_prefix/strip_suffix chain, which will panic if tree-sitter error recovery produces a node without matching single quotes. This is the same error-recovery hazard as in the double-quote case.
issues.suggested_fix: Use `.unwrap_or(text)` to match the established defensive pattern in this codebase, or return `None` on malformed input (e.g. use `?` operator after wrapping in Option).

issues.description: The new `lower_single_quoted_string` method has no call site anywhere in the codebase. The `lower_expr` dispatch table at line ~1697 has no match arm for a single-quoted string node kind, and the tree-sitter grammar (grammar.js lines 805-812) only defines `string_literal` with double quotes. This function is unreachable dead code.
issues.suggested_fix: Either add a grammar rule for single-quoted strings and a corresponding dispatch arm in `lower_expr`, or remove this method if single-quoted strings are not yet supported.

issues.description: The body of `lower_single_quoted_string` is identical to `lower_string_literal` except for the quote character (' vs "). This could be extracted into a shared helper like `strip_quotes(text, quote_char) -> &str` to avoid duplication, especially if more quote styles are added later.
issues.suggested_fix: Extract a helper: `fn strip_outer_quotes(text: &str, quote: char) -> &str` that both methods call.

issues.description: The comment '// Strip outer quotes safely (error recovery can produce malformed nodes)' still exists but the code no longer strips safely — it now panics via `.unwrap()`. The comment and the code are contradictory.
issues.suggested_fix: Either restore the safe unwrap_or (preferred) or update the comment to reflect the new assumption.

comprehensive_reviewer.summary: This diff introduces two blocking regressions and one piece of dead code. The most critical issue is replacing `.unwrap_or(text)` with `.unwrap()` in `lower_string_literal`, which removes a deliberate defensive fallback against tree-sitter error recovery producing malformed nodes — an explicitly documented hazard with an existing test (`unclosed_string_does_not_panic`) that will now fail. The new `lower_single_quoted_string` method has the same `.unwrap()` panic hazard and additionally has no call site: neither the grammar nor the dispatch table reference single-quoted strings, making it unreachable dead code. Both methods also duplicate logic that could share a helper for quote stripping.
